purpose.summary: This COBOL program contains error handling and program termination routines for a CICS environment. It logs errors to a transient data queue and provides a controlled exit from the CICS transaction.

outputs.name: CSSL
outputs.description: Error log records written to the CICS transient data queue CSSL.

called_programs.purpose: Performs termination processing before returning to CICS.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph logs error information to the CICS transient data queue 'CSSL'. It first retrieves the current date and time using CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands, storing them in WS-CUR-DATE-X6 and WS-CUR-TIME-X6 respectively. It then moves the transaction ID (WS-CICS-TRANID) and program ID (WS-PGM-AUTH) into the ERROR-LOG-RECORD. The formatted error record is then written to the 'CSSL' queue. Finally, it checks if the error is critical (ERR-CRITICAL); if so, it performs the 9990-END-ROUTINE to terminate the program.
This paragraph provides a standard exit point from the 9500-LOG-ERROR paragraph. It consists solely of the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph performs the necessary steps to terminate the CICS transaction. It first calls the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph, presumably to perform application-specific cleanup tasks such as closing files or releasing resources. After the termination routine completes, it executes a CICS RETURN command to return control to CICS.
This paragraph provides a standard exit point from the 9990-END-ROUTINE paragraph. It consists solely of the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

cics_operations.purpose: Formats the absolute time into YYMMDD and TIME formats.
Writes an error log record to the transient data queue CSSL.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph?
open_questions.context: The code calls 9000-TERMINATE, but its function is not defined in this snippet.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the source code of 9000-TERMINATE to determine its function.

open_questions.question: What is the structure and content of ERROR-LOG-RECORD?
open_questions.context: The ERROR-LOG-RECORD is written to the CSSL queue, but its fields are not defined in this snippet.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the copybook or data division to determine the structure of ERROR-LOG-RECORD.
